titleStmt.title: Letter from William P. Triston to the Under Secretary of Ireland, 12 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William P. Triston

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from William Triston to the under secretary of Ireland enclosing the petition of Mary Murphy for permission to continue trading while awaiting a new publican's licence.
Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1, Dame Street,
Dublin 12th July 1916
re- Mrs Mary Murphy Sir, I enclose Petition herein and I will
be obliged if you will kindly bring the
Petition forward at the earliest opportunity. I am, Sir,
Your obedient servant William P. Triston The Under Sectretary
Dublin Castle
DUBLIN I Ack & f.a.
II J.h.
For report
Eof
13-VII-16
TO THE LORDS JUSTICES
ACTING FOR THE GOVERNMENT OF IRELAND, DURING
THE VACANCY IN THE OFFICE OF LORD LIEUTENANT
OF IRELAND. May it please your Excellencies. The Petition of Mary Murphy of Duke Street, Athy in the
County of Kildare, sheweth as follows:- Your Petitioner is the owner of a house and premises in
Duke Street, Athy to which was attached a seven-day retail
Publican's Licence. Your Petitioner let the licence and premises with the licence
attached to a Joseph Clarke in September 1914 and in January 1915
he got a Transfer of the Licence at Quarter Sessions in his own
name. The said Joseph Clarke got the usual Renewable Certificate
signed by the Magistrate at the Annual Licencing Petty Sessions
on the 28th day of September 1915 in his own name and very shortly
afterwards he surrendered the premises to your Petitioner. He
did not apply at the Annual Licencing Quarter sessions on the
15th day of October 1915 for the Confirmation of the Transfer
granted to him at the previous Quarter sessions as he had sur-
rendered the premises to your Petitioner and your Petitioner had
not sufficient time in which to apply to the Quarter/Sessions in
October 1915 for a Transfer as the time between the Surrender and
the said Quarter Sessions was too short. The Licence Duty for
the current year has been duly paid but the Excise Authorities
state they cannot issue the Licence in your Petitioners name or
in Joseph Clarke's name, because Joseph Clarke did not get the
Transfer of the Licence confirmed or because your Petitioner did
not apply for a Transfer at the October Quarter Sessions 1915. The Magistrates at Petty Sessions are ready and willing to
grant me a Transfer of the Licence and the Police Authorities
have no objection to the Transfer but as the Excise Authorities
will not issue the Licence, the Magistrates have no document on
which to indorse the Transfer. The next Quarter Sessions for xx
District will not be held until the 20th day of October next and
if your Petitioner is not allowed to trade in the meantime the
Licence will be lost which will mean a considerable loss to your
Petitioner's Income. Your Petitioner therefore prays
that your Excellencies may be
pleased to give your Petitioner
liberty to trade and carry on
the business of Publican in said
licenced house and premises until
the next Annual Licencing Quar-
ter Sessions for the District to
be held at Kildare on the 20th
day of October 1916; and your
Petitioner will ever pray. William P. Triston
Solicitor for Petitioner 1 Dame Street
Dublin 12th July 1916
